skill.name: ui-ux-pro-max
skill.description: Searchable UI and UX reference library for interface design, color palettes, typography, charts, landing pages, and framework guidance.
skill.summary: Plan, review, and implement interface designs using curated UI and UX guidance across common web and mobile stacks.
skill.author: DCjanus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe keyword hits, but targeted review found most were false positives in CSV reference data, documentation URLs, and frontend examples. The skill does require local Python script execution to search bundled files, so it should publish with a medium-risk disclosure rather than a block. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, malicious networking, or destructive filesystem behavior.

medium_findings.title: Local Script Execution Required for Search
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run a bundled Python search script through shell examples. The script appears limited to reading local CSV reference files, but command execution is still an elevated capability that should be disclosed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and CLI entry point are direct evidence of local script execution. The reviewed script routes arguments into local CSV search functions, so malicious intent was not confirmed.

low_findings.title: Environment and Secret Terms Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Static findings for API secrets, database URLs, and environment files occur in framework guidance that warns about safe Next.js environment handling. I did not find evidence that the skill reads or exfiltrates real environment variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged rows are CSV guidance examples and anti-patterns, not executable code. The only executable Python code inspected does not access process environments.

low_findings.title: Network Indicators Are Reference URLs and Snippets
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL and fetch detections are mostly documentation URLs, Google Fonts examples, and frontend teaching snippets. No evidence found of unauthorized outbound requests in executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are static CSV content used for recommendations. They contain URLs and fetch examples, but no executable network client in the Python search path.

low_findings.title: Security Keyword Hits Are False Positives in Design Data
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography, keylogger, C2, and reconnaissance hits map to design words, accessibility examples, framework terminology, or benign product keywords. No evidence found of malicious payloads, prompt injection, credential theft, or command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed benign CSV rows and Chinese/Markdown skill metadata rather than malicious instructions. This confidence is high for sampled representative findings, though not every one of the 688 static hits was manually expanded.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Script Execution Required for Search
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run a bundled Python search script through shell examples. The script appears limited to reading local CSV reference files, but command execution is still an elevated capability that should be disclosed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and CLI entry point are direct evidence of local script execution. The reviewed script routes arguments into local CSV search functions, so malicious intent was not confirmed.

content.user_title: Design Better UI With Searchable UX Guidance
content.value_statement: Interface work often fails because design choices are inconsistent or poorly matched to the product. This skill provides searchable UI, UX, and frontend guidance for faster design decisions.
actual_capabilities: Searches bundled CSV guidance for UI styles, product patterns, colors, typography, charts, landing pages, and UX rules.
Provides stack-specific frontend guidance for React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, and Tailwind.
Helps select color palettes, font pairings, chart types, and layout patterns for specific product categories.
Supports UI review by checking accessibility, responsive layout, hover states, focus states, spacing, and visual polish.
Returns concise recommendations from local data through a Python BM25 search helper.
limitations: The guidance is static and may not reflect the newest framework releases or design trends.
It does not render, screenshot, or automatically test the interface by itself.
Some CSV rows contain example snippets that still require human review before production use.
It relies on local command execution to query the bundled reference data.

use_cases.title: Plan a landing page direction
use_cases.description: Choose a style, palette, typography pair, section order, and conversion guidance before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review a dashboard interface
use_cases.description: Check chart choices, spacing, hierarchy, responsive behavior, and accessibility risks against curated UX guidance.

use_cases.title: Create a design system brief
use_cases.description: Build a practical brief with colors, type, component rules, motion guidance, and stack-specific implementation notes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Style Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max to suggest a visual direction for a healthcare landing page. Include style, colors, typography, and layout priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Component Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this dashboard UI with ui-ux-pro-max. Focus on hierarchy, chart choice, spacing, accessibility, and responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Stack-Specific Implementation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max for a Next.js SaaS dashboard. Provide design guidance and implementation notes for layout, forms, charts, and performance.

prompt_templates.title: Full Design System Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a complete product UI brief.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a design system brief with ui-ux-pro-max for a fintech mobile app. Include style rationale, palette, typography, components, motion, and UX risks.

output_examples.input: Design a premium spa landing page.
output_examples.output: A soft luxury direction with elegant serif headings, calm neutral colors, social proof sections, gentle motion, and accessible contrast checks.

output_examples.input: Plan a mobile education app UI.
output_examples.output: A friendly visual system with rounded controls, clear touch targets, readable typography, simple navigation, and reduced motion support.

best_practices: Search multiple domains before deciding on a final direction.
Validate generated guidance against accessibility and production requirements.
Treat snippets from CSV guidance as examples that need project-specific review.
anti_patterns: Do not copy visual trends without checking product fit and readability.
Do not rely on hover-only interactions for important mobile actions.
Do not ship remote assets or framework snippets without security and performance review.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete UI code?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and examples. The assistant still needs to implement and test the final code.

faq.question: Can it support multiple frontend stacks?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for common web and mobile stacks, including React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, and Tailwind.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: The search helper uses local CSV files. Some reference data contains external documentation and font URLs.

faq.question: Is it safe to use in production projects?
faq.answer: Use it as design guidance. Review all generated implementation details before production release.

faq.question: Can it perform accessibility checks?
faq.answer: It can guide accessibility review, but it does not replace automated audits, screen reader testing, or manual QA.

faq.question: Why does the skill run a Python script?
faq.answer: The script searches bundled reference data and prints matching guidance for the assistant to use.
